equations.label: eq:parallel-trends
equations.description: Parallel trends assumption: counterfactual trends are equal across groups.

equations.label: eq:att
equations.description: Average treatment effect on the treated (ATT) in potential outcomes notation.

equations.label: eq:twfe
equations.description: Two-way fixed effects specification with unit and time fixed effects.

equations.label: eq:event-study
equations.description: Event study specification with leads and lags around treatment date $t^*$.

equations.label: eq:triple-diff
equations.description: Triple difference model with group $g$, county $i$, and time $t$ dimensions.

qa.question: What is the main identification strategy in this paper?
qa.answer: Difference-in-differences exploiting NJ's 1992 minimum wage increase with PA border counties as control

qa.question: What is the main finding for employment?
qa.answer: Employment rates increased by 3.8 percentage points in the treatment group

qa.question: What is the main finding for earnings?
qa.answer: Log weekly earnings increased by 6.2 percent

qa.question: Do pre-trend tests support the parallel trends assumption?
qa.answer: Yes, event study coefficients at tau=-3 and tau=-2 are small and insignificant
